_description: Example hook configurations for retrospect_trigger.py. These hooks are OPTIONAL enhancements — the core trigger mechanism also runs inline in workflow-protocol.md checkpoints.
_platform_note: Adapt the command path and Python executable to your environment. This file is a reference, not a runtime dependency.

hooks.name: retrospect-trigger-after-node-complete
hooks.purpose: After each workflow node completes, scan the result for high-risk signals and retrospective candidates.

hooks.name: retrospect-trigger-after-user-input
hooks.purpose: Detect user corrections and retrospective intent in user messages.

hooks.name: retrospect-trigger-after-review
hooks.purpose: After independent review, scan P0/P1 findings for gate failures that indicate Skill deficiencies.

hooks.name: retrospect-trigger-after-sweep
hooks.purpose: After content consistency sweep, check if recurring inconsistency patterns indicate Skill gaps.

forbidden_behaviors: hooks MUST NOT modify PRD body content
hooks MUST NOT modify any Skill reusable file
hooks MUST NOT bypass user confirmation for Skill changes
hooks MUST NOT auto-apply retrospective patches
hooks MUST NOT elevate a one-time preference to a universal rule without user confirmation

runtime_requirements.dependencies: standard library only (argparse, json, re, datetime)
runtime_requirements.file_write: none — output goes to stdout only
